word_list.pronunciation: pˌʊt ˈɔːl wˈʌnz ˈɛɡz ɪn wˈʌn bˈæskɪt

senses.definition: to only rely on one thing or person for achieving success without any alternatives in case it fails

examples.x: I'm applying for several jobs because I don't really want to put all my eggs in one basket.
Investors should diversify their investments instead of putting all their eggs in one basket.
In today's uncertain job market, it's wise not to put all your eggs in one basket.
To ensure business stability, it's crucial not to put all your eggs in one basket.

word_list.pronunciation: pˌʊt ðə kˈɑːɹt bɪfˌoːɹ ðə hˈɔːɹs

senses.definition: to not do things in order

examples.x: Aren't you putting the cart before the horse by deciding what to wear for the wedding before you've even been invited to it?
You can't start building a house without architectural plans; that's like putting the cart before the horse.
She realized she had put the cart before the horse when she bought a new computer but didn't have the necessary software to use it.
You need to prepare a project proposal before requesting funding; don't put the cart before the horse.
Launching a marketing campaign without understanding your target audience is like putting the cart before the horse.

word_list.pronunciation: ɹˈʌn bɪfˌoːɹ ˌɛsbˈiː kæn kʊd wˈɔːk

senses.definition: to make an attempt to do something difficult without having the requirements or means to do so

examples.x: The important thing about cooking is not to try and run before you can walk.
They tried to run before they could walk.
She wanted to tackle the most challenging tasks at work right away, but her manager advised her not to run before she can walk and focus on learning the basics first.
Starting a business without understanding the industry and market is like running before you can walk; it often leads to failure.

word_list.word: [throw] the baby out with the bathwater
word_list.pronunciation: θɹˈoʊ ðə bˈeɪbi ˈaʊt wɪððə bˈæθwɔːɾɚ

senses.definition: to lose the good alongside the bad in an attempt to dispose of the bad

examples.x: Are you sure that we are not throwing the baby out with the bathwater in dealing with this?
While making changes to the project, be careful not to throw the baby out with the bathwater by discarding valuable ideas in the process.
In revising the company's policies, they need to ensure they don't throw the baby out with the bathwater, losing important elements of the existing structure.

senses.definition: used to say that a person is not interested or concerned about someone or something at all

examples.x: I could not care less about celebrity gossip; it's just not important to me.
She could not care less about the outcome of the sports game, as she's not a fan of either team.
He could not care less if we change the dinner plans; he's fine with anything
When it comes to politics, some people say they could not care less due to the frustration with the system.
I could not care less about the latest fashion trends; I wear what I like and feel comfortable in.

senses.definition: used when a person is not concerned about something or someone

examples.x: You can starve to death for all I care.
You can choose any restaurant for dinner, for all I care.
He can wear whatever clothes he likes, for all I care.
She can take as much time as she wants to finish the project, for all I care.
They can argue about their favorite team all day, for all I care; I'm not interested in sports.

senses.definition: to be concerned about a thing or person

examples.x: I haven't given a shit for the show ever since they killed off my favorite character.
He gives a shit about his health, so he quit smoking and started exercising regularly.
She acts like she doesn't give a shit, but deep down, she really cares about her performance at work.
I give a shit about animal welfare, so I support organizations that work to protect animals.

word_list.pronunciation: ɡˈɪv ɔːɹ kˈɛɹ ɐ tˈɪŋkɚz dˈæm

senses.definition: to express a lack of concern or interest in someone or something

examples.x: As if she gives a tinker's damn what he thinks.
He obviously doesn't give a tinker's damn about what happens.
I don't care a tinker's damn for their opinions.
He acts like he doesn't give a tinker's damn about the outcome, but he's secretly very invested in the project's success."

senses.definition: used to say that something has no negative impact or consequence on someone

examples.x: If he wants to make a mess in his own room, it's no skin off my back.
If you choose not to attend the party, it's no skin off my back; I understand.
He thought that the construction noise would bother his neighbor, but it turned out to be no skin off their backs.
Donating to the charity was no skin off his back; he had the means to help.
Losing the game was no skin off his back; he was just happy to be part of the team.

senses.definition: to not consider a thing or person while planning and fail to prepare a way to deal with them as a result

examples.x: He thought he'd get away with his crime, but he reckoned without the FBI agents.
I know, I really reckoned without my host on that one.
Too many companies reckon without the pitfalls that make insurance like this an absolute necessity.
We'd expected a two-hour drive but had reckoned without the rain.
They thought they could win the competition easily, but they reckoned without the fierce determination of their opponents.
When they made their travel plans, they reckoned without the possibility of flight delays and bad weather.

senses.definition: to decide to do something without considering its consequences

examples.x: Tony accepted the project without realizing how much time it would take; he always dives in headfirst!
She didn't hesitate to dive in headfirst when she was offered a leadership role in the project.
After researching the market, he decided to dive in headfirst and start his own business.
When it comes to learning a new language, it's best to dive in headfirst and practice as much as possible.
They were excited to move to a new country and dive in headfirst to explore a different culture.

senses.definition: to allow a situation or issue to continue without intervention, hoping it become better or resolved in the future

examples.x: Don't panic about the low sales - let it ride for a while and see if business picks up.
He made a rude remark, but I let it ride.
She chose to let his comment ride and not engage in an argument.
Sometimes, it's better to let small issues ride rather than making a big deal out of them.
